notes: Patterns ported from the original Cashiro OldHickoryParser.kt (parser-core/src/main/kotlin/com/ritesh/parser/core/bank/OldHickoryParser.kt) Old Hickory Credit Union (USA) sends account-alert style messages: "A transaction for $27.00 has posted to ACCOUNT NAME (part of ACCOUNT#), which is above the $0.00 value you set." Known deviations from the Kotlin parser (engine cannot express them): - Kotlin prefixes the merchant with "Account: " (e.g. "Account: SAVINGS ACCOUNT") and the reference with "Alert threshold: $". The manifest engine only extracts capture groups, so merchant is the bare account name and reference is the bare threshold amount. - Kotlin returns non-numeric account identifiers (e.g. "ACCOUNT#") verbatim from "(part of ...)"; the engine normalizes accountLast4 to digits, so digit-less identifiers yield no accountLast4.

fixtures.name: standard transaction alert (non-numeric account id)
fixtures.body: A transaction for $27.00 has posted to ACCOUNT NAME (part of ACCOUNT#), which is above the $0.00 value you set.

fixtures.name: numeric sender format
fixtures.body: A transaction for $150.50 has posted to SAVINGS ACCOUNT (part of SAV1234), which is above the $100.00 value you set.

fixtures.name: text sender format
fixtures.body: A transaction for $75.25 has posted to CHECKING ACCOUNT (part of CHK5678), which is above the $50.00 value you set.

fixtures.name: large amount with commas
fixtures.body: A transaction for $1,250.00 has posted to BUSINESS CHECKING (part of BUS9999), which is above the $1,000.00 value you set.

fixtures.name: DLT sender format
fixtures.body: A transaction for $42.99 has posted to CREDIT CARD (part of CC1111), which is above the $25.00 value you set.

fixtures.name: zero threshold
fixtures.body: A transaction for $5.00 has posted to YOUTH SAVINGS (part of YS2222), which is above the $0.00 value you set.

fixtures.name: promotional message is filter-rejected
fixtures.body: Old Hickory CU: Enjoy our new mobile app! Download today and manage your money on the go.
